headline: Newell Brands: Can Cost Cuts Offset Soft Consumer Demand?
summary: NWL launches a productivity plan using AI and automation to counter weak demand and drive cost savings.

headline: BofA urges investors to buy these 3 sectors, not the S&P 500
summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America said in a note Monday that clients should favour energy, consumer staples, and large-cap value stocks over the broader S&P 500, arguing that key risks are not fully reflected in the benchmark’s valuation.

raw.headline: BofA urges investors to buy these 3 sectors, not the S&P 500
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Bank of America said in a note Monday that clients should favour energy, consumer staples, and large-cap value stocks over the broader S&P 500, arguing that key risks are not fully reflected in the benchmark’s valuation.

headline: Airlines target cut on fuel costs but stock declines may be nearing a bottom
summary: Investing.com -- Rising jet fuel prices has UBS analysts cut price targets on U.S. airline stocks, though they say recent share declines may indicate the group is approaching a bottom.

raw.headline: Airlines target cut on fuel costs but stock declines may be nearing a bottom
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Rising jet fuel prices has UBS analysts cut price targets on U.S. airline stocks, though they say recent share declines may indicate the group is approaching a bottom.

headline: UBS cuts price targets on airline stocks amid ’significant uncertainty’
summary: Investing.com -- UBS has lowered estimates and price targets across its U.S. airline coverage, warning of “significant uncertainty” surrounding fuel costs and the sector’s 2026 outlook.

raw.headline: UBS cuts price targets on airline stocks amid ’significant uncertainty’
raw.summary: Investing.com -- UBS has lowered estimates and price targets across its U.S. airline coverage, warning of “significant uncertainty” surrounding fuel costs and the sector’s 2026 outlook.

headline: Capitulation in stocks requires wider credit spreads, Raymond James says
summary: Investing.com -- Stocks continue to decline in what Raymond James describes as an “orderly sell-off,” with major indexes down about 2% over the past week and “little sign of capitulation yet.”

raw.headline: Capitulation in stocks requires wider credit spreads, Raymond James says
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Stocks continue to decline in what Raymond James describes as an “orderly sell-off,” with major indexes down about 2% over the past week and “little sign of capitulation yet.”

headline: The Zacks Analyst Blog Highlights Atmos Energy, Colgate-Palmolive and West Pharmaceutical Services
summary: Dividend Aristocrats like Atmos Energy, Colgate-Palmolive and West Pharmaceutical Services offer steady income as geopolitical tensions and oil price spikes rattle markets.

headline: Ether-linked stocks rise after cryptocurrency hits five-week high
summary: Investing.com -- Stocks tied to Ethereum moved higher on Monday after the cryptocurrency rallied to its highest level in more than five weeks, as a broader crypto surge triggered short liquidations and lifted sentiment across digital assets.

raw.headline: Ether-linked stocks rise after cryptocurrency hits five-week high
raw.summary: Investing.com -- Stocks tied to Ethereum moved higher on Monday after the cryptocurrency rallied to its highest level in more than five weeks, as a broader crypto surge triggered short liquidations and lifted sentiment across digital assets.

headline: Assessing Colgate Palmolive (CL) Valuation After Recent Choppy Trading And Longer Term Shareholder Returns
summary: Why Colgate-Palmolive (CL) Is Drawing Investor Attention Now Colgate-Palmolive (CL) is back on many investors’ screens after a recent pullback, with the stock showing a negative return over the past month but a positive move across the past 3 months. That split performance, combined with annual revenue of US$20.38b and net income of US$2.13b, is prompting closer scrutiny of how this long-standing consumer products group is currently valued. See our latest analysis for...
